best_trajectory.thought: The search results from authoritative fact-checking platforms (e.g., China Internet Joint Fact-Checking Platform, Shenzhen News) confirm that the rumor of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda being dismantled for major repairs is false. The provided text accurately reflects the official response by labeling the rumor as false and describing the actual ongoing measures (monitoring, surveying, partial reinforcement), which aligns with the search findings.

The news text describes a protection project for the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda focused on monitoring, surveying, and "local reinforcement" (局部加固). It emphasizes "safety and original state protection." The image explicitly states in the title that news of "complete dismantling for major repairs" is false and clarifies that experts advocate for "only local protective repairs" (仅进行局部保护修缮). The image also visually shows scaffolding and barriers in the background of the pagoda, confirming the mention of construction elements. Furthermore, the image shows a media report "blitz" and interview, providing the visual context for the "authoritative release" claiming the dismantling rumor is false. The content of the image aligns perfectly with the clarifying protection strategy described in the text.
